title: Bump @tanstack/react-query 5.99.2 → 5.100.10 (manual PR, FE-only)

acCompliance.rationale: All actionable spec ACs satisfied. (1) `museum-frontend/package.json:40-41` verbatim now reads `"@tanstack/react-query": "^5.100.10"` + `"@tanstack/react-query-persist-client": "^5.100.10"` (grep confirmed: 2 matches at lines 40-41). (2) Lockfile `museum-frontend/package-lock.json` regenerated coherently: top-level packages block (line 23) + node_modules/@tanstack/react-query (lines 4955-4970, version 5.100.10, integrity sha512 FLaZf2RCrA/...) + node_modules/@tanstack/react-query-persist-client (lines 4971-4987, version 5.100.10, peer-dep `@tanstack/react-query: ^5.100.10`) + transitive query-core 5.100.10 (lines 4988-4996, 5011-5019) + query-persist-client-core 5.100.10 (lines 4998-5009). (3) `npm run lint` exit 0 (eslint --max-warnings=0 + tsc --noEmit incremental). (4) `npm test` exit 0, 267 suites pass / 2825 tests pass / 4 snapshots pass — no Jest regressions, matches green-agent claim verbatim. (5) `npm audit` clean (0 vulnerabilities across all severities). (6) musaium-shared 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 sync in lockfile (line 110) is expected per CLAUDE.md `@musaium/shared file: dep` gotcha — packages/musaium-shared/package.json version verified at 0.2.0 (T1.5 bump). -4 only because the spec § Task T1.10.f (CHANGELOG note) was explicitly deferred per Open Question Q3 (CHANGELOG.md absent in museum-frontend/, convention unclear) and AC YAML mentions "CHANGELOG.md note" — partial AC by acknowledged design choice (UFR-013 honest deferral), not a hidden gap.

scopeFidelity.rationale: git diff --stat HEAD -- museum-frontend/package.json museum-frontend/package-lock.json = exactly 2 files changed (4 insertions / 4 deletions on package.json, 47 insertions / 14 deletions on lockfile). Zero collateral edits to backend, web, design-system, infra, scripts, or other museum-frontend source files. Anti-collision chat-ux contract verified: `git diff --name-only HEAD museum-frontend/features/chat/` returns empty — `useCompareImage.ts` (the single chat consumer of `useMutation`/`UseMutationResult` per spec § Anti-collision) is untouched. The worktree contains many other in-progress S1 task files (CLAUDE.md, env.ts, museum-backend/*, packages/musaium-shared/dist/*) but ALL are outside T1.10's declared scope and belong to sibling tasks (T1.5, T1.9, etc.). Diff filtering to the 2 T1.10 files is strict and clean.

kissDry.rationale: Textbook minor-version bump within same major (5.99 → 5.100): mechanical, surgical, no code refactor invited or attempted. Both `@tanstack/react-query` and `@tanstack/react-query-persist-client` bumped in lockstep (spec § Étapes #1 satisfied — peer-deps coherent: persist-client now demands `^5.100.10`, matches direct dep). Lockfile regeneration is minimal — only the 2 direct packages + 2 mandatory transitives (`query-core` + `query-persist-client-core` at 5.100.10, no other unrelated bumps). Verified via `git diff HEAD -- museum-frontend/package-lock.json | grep -E '^\+\s+"version":' | sort -u` → exactly 2 distinct version strings (`5.100.10` + `0.2.0`), zero collateral. No scope creep, no piling of unrelated upgrades.

a11yDesign.rationale: N/A — pure dependency bump, no UI/UX/a11y surface. Default 95 per task contract. No screen edits, no token changes, no copy changes.

securityHonesty.rationale: Every file:line citation verified by Read: package.json:40-41 ✓ (grep '@tanstack/react-query' confirms ^5.100.10 on both packages), package-lock.json:23 top-level ref ✓, package-lock.json:108-117 musaium-shared 0.2.0 sync ✓, package-lock.json:4955-5020 the 5 lockfile entries (react-query + persist-client + 2 transitive query-core copies + query-persist-client-core) all at 5.100.10 with new sha512 integrities ✓. `npm audit --json` returns `{info: 0, low: 0, moderate: 0, high: 0, critical: 0, total: 0}` — zero new CVEs introduced. No breaking change in minor bump (TanStack semver compliant, spec § Breaking changes confirmed via the narrow API surface: useQuery / useMutation / useQueryClient / keepPreviousData / PersistQueryClientProvider — all stable in 5.x). Honesty checks: green-agent's claim of 2825 tests PASS reproduced verbatim (`npm test` exit 0, captured to /tmp/t1.10-test.log). Test runner does emit one 'A worker process has failed to exit gracefully' warning — this is a pre-existing Jest open-handle pattern across the FE suite (not introduced by this bump, exit code remains 0, all 267 suites pass). -3 only because the spec's smoke check (§ Tasks T1.10.g: `npm run dev` → chat session list, museum directory paginé, settings TTS) is a MANUAL step deferred to the user — not verifiable from the diff alone; the review confirms the automated portion only, and that limitation is stated transparently here (UFR-013).

niceToHave.title: CHANGELOG note skip (Open Question Q3 deferral)
niceToHave.detail: Spec § Tasks T1.10.f notes the AC YAML mentions "CHANGELOG.md note" but `museum-frontend/CHANGELOG.md` does not exist (only `packages/musaium-shared/CHANGELOG.md` exists in the repo). The green-agent appears to have applied Open Question Q3 option (a) — skip CHANGELOG step. This is acknowledged in the spec and is the correct honest choice (UFR-013 over silent gap), but the commit body (or PR description when manual PR is opened) should mention 'react-query ^5.99.2 → ^5.100.10 (+ persist-client lockstep)' so the bump is traceable in `git log` until the FE CHANGELOG convention is clarified. Not blocking — purely a documentation traceability nice-to-have.
niceToHave.action: Optional: include `chore(deps,fe): bump @tanstack/react-query 5.99.2 → 5.100.10 (+ persist-client lockstep)` as the canonical commit subject so the bump is grep-able in git history.

niceToHave.title: Manual smoke (spec § T1.10.g) deferred to user
niceToHave.detail: Spec requires `npm run dev` manual smoke: chat session list charge OK / museum directory paginé / settings TTS voice save. Review covers automated checks only (lint + test + audit + diff scope). This is a normal split (review-agent cannot drive an Expo dev server interactively) but worth flagging so the orchestrator/user knows the manual portion is still outstanding before merge. Since 5.99 → 5.100 is a minor bump with the narrow API surface listed in spec § Consumers (useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient, keepPreviousData, PersistQueryClientProvider — all stable), the risk of behavioural regression is low.
niceToHave.action: Orchestrator: prompt user to run `cd museum-frontend && npm run dev`, open chat session list + museum directory + settings TTS voice save before merging. If user opts to skip, document the skip + risk acceptance in PR description.

niceToHave.title: Open Question Q1 (latest 5.x) — confirm 5.100.10 was actually latest non-major at green time
niceToHave.detail: Spec § Open Question Q1 advises taking the latest 5.x at T+0 via `npm view @tanstack/react-query versions --json`. The green-agent landed on 5.100.10 (matches the spec's preliminary claim). This is consistent with the lockfile resolution timestamp embedded in the diff, but the review-agent did not independently re-run `npm view` to confirm no 5.100.11+ shipped between spec authorship and green-agent execution. Given the 24-48h window between spec and green, the gap is bounded; if a 5.100.11 patch shipped, it can be picked up by Renovate later (this manual PR was specifically to catch up the ~7 accumulated patches, so one extra patch loop is acceptable). Not blocking.
niceToHave.action: Backlog (optional): re-check `npm view @tanstack/react-query@5 dist-tags.latest` post-merge; if newer 5.x ship, let Renovate handle it as designed.

verificationLog.diffStat: git diff --stat HEAD -- museum-frontend/package.json museum-frontend/package-lock.json → 2 files changed, 47 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-). Net: +33 LoC (lockfile expansion for the 2 new transitive @tanstack/query-core + query-persist-client-core blocks).

lockfileBumps.transitiveQueryCore: package-lock.json:4988-4996 + 5011-5019 → 2 nested copies of @tanstack/query-core@5.100.10 (npm's nested-resolution pattern, expected)

musaiumSharedSync.rationale: Expected per CLAUDE.md gotcha `@musaium/shared file: dep` — T1.5 bumped the shared package, and any `npm install` in museum-frontend rematerializes the file: dep version. Mechanical, not a scope violation.

noUnrelatedBumps.command: git diff HEAD -- museum-frontend/package-lock.json | grep -E '^\+\s+"version":' | sort -u
noUnrelatedBumps.result: +      "version": "0.2.0", (musaium-shared sync, expected) | +      "version": "5.100.10", (the 5 @tanstack/* entries, expected) — ZERO other version bumps in lockfile.

antiCollisionChatUx.result: (empty) — useCompareImage.ts intact, no chat-ux branch collision possible. Anti-collision contract respected.

feLint.result: eslint --cache --max-warnings=0 + tsc --noEmit --incremental → both pass, no warnings, no type errors.

feTest.result: Test Suites: 267 passed, 267 total | Tests: 2825 passed, 2825 total | Snapshots: 4 passed, 4 total | Time: 33.731s. Matches green-agent claim verbatim. One Jest warning 'A worker process has failed to exit gracefully' — pre-existing FE-wide open-handle pattern (not introduced by this bump, suite still passes).

npmAudit.result: {info: 0, low: 0, moderate: 0, high: 0, critical: 0, total: 0} — 0 vulnerabilities at all severities.

verdictNotes: APPROVED with 3 nice-to-have follow-ups. Textbook minor-version bump: scope strict (2 files), lockfile coherent (only @tanstack/* + expected musaium-shared sync, zero unrelated drift), all gates green (lint exit 0, 2825/2825 tests pass, npm audit clean). Anti-collision chat-ux contract fully respected (features/chat/ untouched). The 3 follow-ups (CHANGELOG note deferral, manual smoke deferred to user, latest 5.x re-check) are all transparently flagged for traceability — no hidden gaps, UFR-013 honored throughout. Ready to commit.
